concept.conceptName: Phosphate | Serum or Plasma | Chemistry - non-challenge

en.name: Phosphate in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Phosphate (inorganic) is one of the routine electrolytes; its concentration in blood is measured to investigate hypo- or hyperphosphataemia, particularly in critical care, kidney disease, and refeeding syndrome [1]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a biochemistry analyser. Results are reported as a substance concentration in mmol/L (SI unit) or as a mass concentration in mg/dL [2]. ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on two LOINC Hierarchy concepts (one per blood specimen, scoped to `Chemistry - non-challenge`), with their descendants pulled in. The set resolves to seven LOINC "Lab Test" concepts, grouped here by specimen. ### Serum or plasma (default specimen for routine biochemistry) - *3003458 / 14879-1 — Phosphate [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: most widely used LOINC code worldwide. - *3011904 / 2777-1 — Phosphate [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: same measurand expressed as mass concentration. - *3050450 / 48617-5 — Phosphate [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma --post dialysis (LOINC)*: point-in-time measurement immediately after a haemodialysis session. - *3049552 / 48641-5 — Phosphate [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma --pre dialysis (LOINC)*: point-in-time measurement immediately before a haemodialysis session. - *40759093 / 55972-4 — Phosphate [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma --post dialysis (LOINC)*: same as `48617-5` expressed as substance concentration. ### Generic blood (specimen unspecified) - *3018913 / 24519-1 — Phosphate [Moles/volume] in Blood (LOINC)* - *3024232 / 2774-8 — Phosphate [Mass/volume] in Blood (LOINC)*: same measurand expressed as mass concentration. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`mmol/L`** (concept 8753 — millimole per litre) for `[Moles/volume]` codes and **`mg/dL`** (concept 8840) for `[Mass/volume]` codes. Conversion: `1 mmol/L ≈ 3.10 mg/dL` (molar mass of phosphorus = 30.974 g/mol; the LOINC "Phosphate" component refers to inorganic phosphate, reported as elemental phosphorus). ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded by name. The set deliberately omits the following LOINC concepts: - **Red Blood Cells specimens**: intra-erythrocytic phosphate, specialised research measurement. - **Urine, CSF, body fluid and other specimens**: out of scope for a "blood" concept set. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "phosphate", "phosphorus", "P", "PO4", "phosphataemia" without further specification: `3003458 / 14879-1 — Phosphate [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma`. By far the most common LOINC code. - **Source unit is mg/dL**: convert to mmol/L using `1 mg/dL ≈ 0.3229 mmol/L` (i.e. divide by 3.0974). - **Source labels the analyte as "phosphorus"**: identical to "phosphate" in this context — both refer to the inorganic phosphate concentration expressed as elemental phosphorus. - **Do not confuse with**: organic phosphate (phospholipids, ATP, etc.), urinary phosphate, intra-erythrocytic phosphate. ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 14879-1 — Phosphate [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma. Available: https://loinc.org/14879-1/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
